name: Steadybit Chaos Engineering API
what_it_does: Modern cloud-native chaos engineering platform with REST API — providing experiment design, execution, and analysis with Kubernetes-native attack extensions, OpenTelemetry integration, and an extension hub for custom fault types and observability correlation.
use_cases: Designing and running chaos experiments via API for automated reliability testing
Correlating chaos experiment results with observability data (metrics, traces)
Running Kubernetes chaos experiments (pod deletion, network partition) via API
Integrating chaos experiment status into CI/CD pipelines for reliability gates
Scheduling automated game days and resilience validation via Steadybit API
not_for: Simple load testing without failure injection
Monitoring and observability (Steadybit integrates with, not replaces, observability tools)
best_when: A Kubernetes-native team wants modern chaos engineering with OpenTelemetry correlation, extension hub for custom attacks, and API-driven experiment automation.
avoid_when: You need enterprise chaos engineering with long-established enterprise support (use Gremlin), or need non-Kubernetes chaos beyond basic cloud.

auth.notes: API access token from platform settings. Bearer token auth. Team-scoped access. Read and write scopes for experiment management. Webhook for experiment completion events. OpenAPI spec available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 10 agents, basic chaos experiments
pricing.notes: Free tier for small teams. Agent-based pricing. German company (Hamburg) — GDPR-first. EU data residency. On-premise and SaaS options. Extension hub for community and custom attacks. OpenTelemetry integration differentiator. Growing k8s chaos engineering adoption.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with OpenAPI spec for experiments, environments, targets, and extensions. Bearer token auth. Webhook for experiment events. Good documentation for modern cloud-native patterns. Extension hub enables custom attack types. OpenTelemetry integration for observability correlation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Experiment execution creates new runs — not idempotent. Experiment definition management idempotent by ID. Target discovery reads idempotent. Report generation creates new artifacts.
known_agent_gotchas: Steadybit agent must be installed on target infrastructure
Extension hub extensions must be configured before using custom attack types
OpenTelemetry correlation requires configuring observability backend connection
German company — EU data residency by default; US SaaS available

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. German company — EU-first data practices. Bearer token with scope separation. EU and US data residency. Agent-based architecture — attacks run locally. On-premise option for maximum data control.
